steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Left of the blank: subject noun phrase (“the plant's chloroplasts”). Right of the blank: noun phrase serving as an appositive (“the cellular organs that generate energy from light”).

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content: Appositives in the middle of a sentence must be set off with matching punctuation on both sides—since the text shows an em dash before “reshuffled,” we need an opening em dash after “chloroplasts.” The other choices violate this rule: a comma doesn't match the em dash, a semicolon incorrectly signals a clause boundary, and no punctuation creates ambiguity between adjacent noun phrases. That matches choice B — B is correct.
